modules.title: Crimson Desert Release Date and Platforms
modules.summary: Crimson Desert is available now. It launched on March 19, 2026 as a single-player open-world action adventure across current-gen consoles, PC, Mac, and cloud play.

cards.title: Release Status
cards.detail: Players can buy and start the campaign immediately.

cards.title: Launch Date
cards.detail: The retail release followed the September 2025 reveal window.

cards.title: Offline Play
cards.detail: Offline play works after initial setup and the day one patch.

cards.title: Cross-Save
cards.detail: Progress does not move between platforms.

items.detail: PC ecosystem support.

items.title: Mac App Store
items.detail: Dedicated Mac purchase path.

items.title: GeForce NOW
items.detail: Cloud streaming access.

modules.note: PC controller support includes Xbox controllers and DualSense over USB.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Trailer Hub
modules.summary: Start with the official launch trailer, then work backward through the release date reveal, feature videos, and long-form gameplay showcases to understand the game fast.

items.title: Official Launch Trailer
items.detail: The current best snapshot of the release build and campaign tone.

items.title: Release Date Reveal Trailer
items.detail: The September 24, 2025 State of Play announcement that locked the release window.

items.detail: Best primer for the setting, travel scale, and narrative direction.

items.title: Feature Video #2: Combat and Progression

modules.note: The official playlist also keeps earlier showcase trailers like gamescom ONL 2023 and The Game Awards 2020 in one archive.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Beginner Guide
modules.summary: The first hours revolve around Hernand, early Greymane quests, weapon-driven combat, and the town-to-camp loop of gathering, crafting, cooking, and unlocking stronger tools.

items.title: Reach Hernand and stabilize the early loop
items.detail: Help townspeople, clear outlaw-held spots, and open up more activities and quest lines.

items.title: Learn the basic attack rhythm first
items.detail: Chain fast and heavy attacks, then layer in blocking, parrying, grabs, and throws.

items.title: Visit the early vendors that matter
items.detail: The smithy, equipment shop, tailor, tannery, grocer, butchery, and fishing shop all help define your early momentum.

items.title: Prioritize gathering tools and cooking
items.detail: A fishing rod, pickaxe, and logging axe open the resource loop that feeds crafting and camp growth.

modules.note: Abyss Artifacts are the key early upgrade currency for unlocking stronger abilities and combat options.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Story Explained
modules.summary: Crimson Desert follows Kliff and the Greymanes across Pywel after the Black Bears shatter the fragile balance of Pailune, scattering the survivors across the continent.

items.title: Pywel is the campaign stage
items.detail: Cities, wilderness, ruins, and the Abyss all shape the route and stakes of the story.

items.title: Kliff leads the Greymanes
items.detail: Oongka, Yann, and Naira remain central names in the scattered survivor story.

items.title: The Black Bears trigger the collapse
items.detail: Their ambush breaks the balance in Pailune and forces the surviving Greymanes into recovery and retaliation.

items.title: The Abyss expands the scope
items.detail: The story grows beyond simple revenge as hidden factions and supernatural forces come into view.

modules.note: Additional playable perspectives later in the journey include Oongka and Damiane.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Characters
modules.summary: The cast is anchored by Kliff and the Greymanes, then widened through allies, rival factions, and later playable perspectives that deepen the campaign.

items.title: Kliff
items.detail: The central viewpoint character and battlefield anchor for the campaign.

items.title: Oongka
items.detail: A Greymane companion who also becomes playable later in the story.

items.title: Naira
items.detail: A core Greymane survivor tied closely to the faction's recovery arc.

items.title: Yann
items.detail: Another important Greymane name to track as the scattered band regroups.

items.title: Damiane
items.detail: A later playable perspective who broadens the campaign structure.

items.title: Black Bears
items.detail: The antagonistic force that turns faction instability into open conflict.

modules.note: This module is built as a cast overview first, with room to expand into later character and faction profile pages.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Open World and Regions
modules.summary: Pywel mixes cities, wild frontiers, ruins, and supernatural space into an exploration loop driven by landmarks, hidden spots, quests, and combat points of interest.

items.title: Cities
items.detail: Urban hubs act as story anchors, merchant clusters, and route reset points.

items.title: Wilderness
items.detail: Open traversal space supports combat encounters, gathering, and discovery-heavy travel.

items.title: Ruins
items.detail: Ancient structures frame puzzle solving, environmental storytelling, and hidden rewards.

items.title: The Abyss
items.detail: A floating and otherworldly space that shifts the campaign beyond grounded frontier conflict.

items.title: Landmarks
items.detail: Named locations guide navigation and turn broad regions into recognizable routes.

items.title: Exploration Points
items.detail: Combat spots, side activities, and hidden objectives reward players who leave the main trail.

modules.note: This homepage block works as a regional overview before dedicated Pywel subpages exist.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Combat Guide
modules.summary: Crimson Desert combat emphasizes weapon-driven rhythm, close-range pressure, strong defensive reads, and precise reactions against heavier enemies and bosses.

items.title: Fast and heavy attacks work as the core rhythm
items.detail: Use them together to control tempo instead of spamming a single string.

items.title: Blocking and parrying decide high-pressure fights
items.detail: The game rewards tight defensive timing when enemy aggression spikes.

items.title: Grabs and throws widen the action set
items.detail: The toolkit is broader than a basic light-heavy combo loop.

items.title: Enemy reading matters as much as damage output
items.detail: Spacing, reaction windows, and counterplay define higher-pressure encounters.

modules.note: This module is tuned for players searching for core combat feel rather than late-game optimization spreadsheets.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Weapons and Skills
modules.summary: There is no fixed class system. Your fighting style changes through weapon choice, learned moves, and the way upgrades expand your combat toolkit.

items.title: Sword and Shield
items.detail: The early anchor set for players learning defense, positioning, and control.

items.title: Greatswords
items.detail: A heavier option for broader swings, weightier pressure, and commitment-based offense.

items.title: Spears
items.detail: Useful when reach, precision, and spacing matter more than raw brawling tempo.

items.title: Axes
items.detail: A direct route into stronger impact and punishing exchanges.

items.title: Ranged Options
items.detail: Supplement melee pressure with distance control and encounter flexibility.

items.title: Abyss Artifact Upgrades
items.detail: Upgrade currency that unlocks stronger abilities and new skill expression.

modules.note: The best way to read the system is by weapons and movesets, not by traditional RPG class labels.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Boss Guide
modules.summary: Boss coverage on the homepage starts as a preparation-focused overview: how encounters look, how pressure changes across phases, and what players should bring into major fights.

items.title: Preparation matters before damage racing
items.detail: The game pushes players to arrive with the right rhythm, tools, and defensive habits.

items.title: Boss identity is highly visual
items.detail: Silhouette, arena pressure, and move readability all help define the fight.

items.title: Phase shifts change tempo
items.detail: Encounters reward adaptation instead of one solved pattern from start to finish.

items.title: Guide pages can split later by individual boss
items.detail: Homepage coverage is built as a launch pad for deeper single-boss walkthroughs.

modules.note: This block is intentionally light on spoilers and heavy on practical expectations for major encounters.

modules.title: Crimson Desert Multiplayer and Offline
modules.summary: This is one of the most common launch questions, so the homepage answers it directly: Crimson Desert is a single-player game with offline support after initial setup.

cards.title: Multiplayer
cards.detail: Crimson Desert is not a co-op or online multiplayer release.

cards.title: Single Player
cards.detail: The core experience is built around a solo campaign.

cards.title: Offline After Setup
cards.detail: Players can continue offline after first-time setup and the day one patch.

cards.title: Cross-Save
cards.detail: There is no supported cross-platform save migration.

modules.note: This module exists specifically for searches like 'Is Crimson Desert multiplayer?' and 'Can Crimson Desert be played offline?'

faq.title: Crimson Desert Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers for the launch questions players keep asking.

items.question: What kind of game is Crimson Desert?
items.answer: Crimson Desert is a single-player open-world action adventure centered on Kliff, the Greymanes, and the continent of Pywel.

items.question: Is Crimson Desert multiplayer or co-op?
items.answer: Crimson Desert is not a multiplayer or co-op game. It is built as a single-player campaign.

items.question: Can Crimson Desert be played offline?
items.answer: Crimson Desert supports offline play after initial setup and the day one patch are completed.

items.question: Does Crimson Desert support cross-save?
items.answer: Crimson Desert does not support cross-save between platforms.

items.question: Who is the main character in Crimson Desert?
items.answer: Kliff is the central lead in Crimson Desert, with the Greymanes forming the core faction around him.

items.question: What should I learn first in Crimson Desert combat?
items.answer: In Crimson Desert, start by learning the rhythm between fast attacks, heavy attacks, blocking, parrying, and movement under pressure.

items.question: Where should I track official Crimson Desert updates?
items.answer: Use the official notices page, the official YouTube channel, the official X account, and the official Discord for ongoing Crimson Desert updates.
